title: Project Report: Home Lab Security Hardening — Cybersecurity Analyst
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Harden a Linux VM: firewall, SSH keys, fail2ban, updates, and document baseline checklist.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**Home Lab Security Hardening** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Cybersecurity Analyst. Duration: 2 weeks. Harden a Linux VM: firewall, SSH keys, fail2ban, updates, and document baseline checklist. Tools: Linux, UFW, fail2ban, nmap.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: UFW firewall rules configured
Objective: SSH key-only auth (no passwords)
Objective: fail2ban installed
Objective: Security baseline checklist completed
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Cybersecurity Analyst portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Cybersecurity Analyst resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Cybersecurity Analyst resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Baseline
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Baseline** *(Level: Beginner)*

Document current state. Complete these tasks: Open ports scan; User accounts. Deliverable for this phase: Baseline audit.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Open ports scan
- [ ] User accounts
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Baseline is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Cybersecurity Analyst agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Baseline audit to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: Harden
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Harden** *(Level: Beginner)*

Apply CIS basics. Complete these tasks: UFW; SSH keys; fail2ban. Deliverable for this phase: Hardened VM.

Code Reference:
```text

- [ ] SSH keys
- [ ] fail2ban
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

chapters.title: Phase: Verify
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Verify** *(Level: Beginner)*

Re-scan after hardening. Complete these tasks: nmap scan; SSH test. Deliverable for this phase: Before/after comparison.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] nmap scan
- [ ] SSH test
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Verify is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 3 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Cybersecurity Analyst agile sprints and coursework milestones.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: Baseline audit; Hardening runbook; Scan comparison. Skills demonstrated: Linux security, Hardening. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Baseline audit
Deliverable: Hardening runbook
Deliverable: Scan comparison
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Baseline audit and passes the rubric.
